marketState: neutral, tilting bearish near-term with oversold bounce risk
summary: Core seasonality inputs (vsMedian, probability-higher, typical 4-week move, reliability) are all null in this snapshot, so the top-priority signal is unavailable and any medium-term call is low-conviction. Weather demand is running mildly above the 5-year normal (+5.3% over 1-7D, +0.9% over 8-16D, trend +3% vs normal) during cooling season — modestly supportive but far from extreme, with no above-max readings. Front price ($3.008) sits essentially at fair value ($2.917-$2.980 across horizons, FAIR status, spreads only +$0.03 to +$0.09) inside a wide $2.42-$3.48 band, so there is no valuation edge in either direction. Storage printed a bearish surprise (+72 Bcf vs forecast, +184 Bcf surplus vs 5y) and technicals are bearish-aligned (50<200 EMA cross and bearish trend on all six timeframes, MACD negative on 4 of 6), but RSI is deeply oversold intraday (20.4 on 1h, 22.8 on 4h) with price below the lower Bollinger band on 4h/1d, raising the risk of a near-term relief bounce. Managed money is net short but only at the 31st percentile of its 5-year range, so positioning isn't stretched enough to flag an imminent short-covering squeeze, and the futures curve is flat-to-slightly backwardated into fall before stepping into a normal winter contango.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (vsMedian, probHigher, typicalMove4w, reliability) is entirely missing, removing the top-priority medium-term input, and the remaining evidence is mixed: mildly supportive weather demand (+3% vs normal) and non-extreme COT positioning (31st percentile short) versus a bearish storage surplus/surprise, while price sits at FAIR value with no band-edge edge.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for seasonality/vsMedian data to populate and for either price to reach a fair-value band edge (below ~$2.60 for long interest, above ~$3.30 for short interest) or for storage deviation vs 5y to clearly widen or narrow, before sizing a 3-12 week position.

shortTerm.entry: $3.00 - $3.05 (sell current level / any bounce toward resistance)
shortTerm.rationale: All six technical timeframes are bearish-aligned (50<200 EMA cross) with a fresh bearish storage surprise (+72 Bcf vs forecast) just printed and MACD negative on 4 of 6 timeframes; near-term weather (+5.3% vs 5y) isn't strong enough to offset the technical/storage bearishness while price sits at fair value with no cheapness to buy.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close back above $3.20 (through the fair-value spread and reclaiming the 50/200 EMA structure) or a bullish storage/weather surprise would invalidate the short thesis.
